monitoring.alert_warning: p95 > 2.7s for 5 consecutive minutes
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 3s sustained for 10 minutes OR p99 > 5s for any request
monitoring.alert_incident: workspace creation SLA breach; >1% of requests exceed 5s

mitigation_automation: auto-scale web tier if CPU >70%
reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-001 budgets p95 3s, p99 5s. Applicant workspace is entry-point journey; SLA breach triggers abandonment. Synthetic test simulates auth + workspace init + UI render every 60 seconds (OFFICIAL tier path only).
recommendation: Deploy synthetic test from two edge locations (UK-South, UK-North). Inject synthetic test traffic evenly across day/night to avoid false positives on baseline latency shifts. Alert thresholds: warning at 90% of budget (2.7s), critical at 100% budget.

component: Evidence upload (100 MB pack, per-request RUM)

monitoring.alert_warning: p95 upload latency > 27s (90% of 30s budget) for OFFICIAL tier; trending toward timeout
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 30s OR any request >40s; evidence_upload SLA breach
monitoring.alert_incident: >5% of uploads fail or timeout; application queue depth >100 pending uploads

mitigation_automation: circuit-break if evidence_store write latency >5s; drop new uploads to queue with user-visible retry countdown
reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-002 budgets p95 30s for 100 MB pack. Network upload dominates (8s budget). RUM captures real applicant network conditions (4G, hotel wifi variable). Tier-specific: cannot monitor SECRET tier via RUM (no applicant access); OFFICIAL only.
recommendation: Inject RUM beacon on file upload completion. Segment by network type (broadband vs 4G detected). Track upload throughput (MB/s) as secondary metric. Alert on anomalies in virus-scan latency (>3s suggests malware signature DB issue).

component: Coverage matrix generation (APM on inference tier)

monitoring.alert_warning: p95 matrix generation > 540s (90% of 600s budget); GPU queue depth trending >5
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 600s OR p99 > 720s; matrix SLA breach for 100 MB typical case
monitoring.alert_incident: Inference error rate >2%; GPU worker crashes causing job loss

mitigation_automation: auto-scale GPU workers: add 1 instance if queue depth >20; shed oldest low-priority batch if queue >50
reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-003 budgets 600s (10 min) p95 for 100 MB. Spans OCR (180s), semantic classification (200s), matrix computation (90s). APM traces inference component latency. Largest budget component; GPU saturation is key bottleneck. Cold retention for 1 year supports post-trial lessons-learned analysis (cluster similar inference patterns across cases).
recommendation: Instrument each GPU worker with Prometheus metrics: queue_depth, inference_latency_histogram, ocr_latency, semantic_classification_latency. Alert if any single inference job stalls >180s (watchdog timer). Monitor GPU memory utilization; alert if >90% sustained (batch-size tuning needed).

component: AI-flag narrative service (APM on LLM tier)

monitoring.alert_warning: p95 narrative latency > 1620s (90% of 1800s budget); GPU inference backlog >10 requests
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 1800s; narrative SLA breach OR inference timeout >30s on single chunk
monitoring.alert_incident: LLM model serving crash; inference error rate >5%; regulator complaint about narrative quality

mitigation_automation: auto-scale vLLM instances if batch queue >5 requests; circuit-break token generation if latency spike >50%
reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-007 budgets 1800s (30 min) p95 for narrative generation. Evidence chunking (60s) + batch inference (900s) + narrative aggregation (120s) + linking (240s) + rendering (480s). LLM inference is GPU-bottlenecked and tokenization-sensitive. One-shot batch operation per case review; regulators expect 'flags within 30 min'. Streaming token delivery prevents regulator abandonment (partial narratives shown as chunks complete).
recommendation: Track tokens/second and batch throughput. Monitor model serving framework (vLLM) for OOM errors (context window exhaustion on very large evidence packs). Alert if any single narrative request exceeds 2000s (hard deadline). Log inference errors with chunk ID for root-cause analysis (malformed evidence, encoding issues).

monitoring.alert_warning: p95 case-open latency > 9s (90% of 10s budget) for OFFICIAL tier; regulator perception of slowness begins
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 10s OR p99 > 15s; case-open SLA breach impacts panel productivity
monitoring.alert_incident: Error rate >1% on case-open; cases failing to load entirely (DB or evidence-store outage)

mitigation_automation: add DB read replica if p95 latency >8s; prefetch next 3 likely cases from case list
reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-006 budgets 10s p95 for case assembly + UI render. Regulators on classified network (low latency LAN); case-open is high-frequency (~10-20 cases per panel day). RUM captures real regulator experience including network variance. Classification-aware: SECRET tier (air-gapped) will have higher latency; separate SLA (15s acceptable).
recommendation: Inject RUM beacon on case-open completion. Segment by classification tier (OFFICIAL vs OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE vs SECRET). Track case-assembly latency (app server) vs evidence-metadata-load separately. Alert if >5% of cases fail to load (evidence archive outage signal).

monitoring.alert_warning: p95 navigation latency > 1.8s (90% of 2s budget) during panel sessions; regulator flow interrupted
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 2s sustained; high-frequency interactivity SLA breach
monitoring.alert_incident: >5% of evidence links fail to navigate (cache invalidation issue or broken references)

mitigation_automation: none (UI-side optimization; cache already hot)
reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-008 budgets 2s p99 for cached evidence navigation. High-frequency operation during regulator panel work (100+ clicks per case). Cache hit probability must be >95%. DOM rendering dominates (0.8s budget). RUM is essential here; backend latency is not bottleneck.
recommendation: Monitor cache hit rate per evidence section. Alert if cache hit rate drops <90% (evidence modification during deliberation, or cache eviction due to size pressure). Track DOM rendering time separately (JavaScript profiling on client side); alert if >800ms (browser-side bottleneck).

monitoring.alert_warning: p95 action-log write latency > 450ms (90% of 500ms budget); quorum replication slowing
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 500ms OR any write >750ms; audit-trail SLA breach (REQ012 compliance risk)
monitoring.alert_incident: Log write error rate >0.1%; distributed log replication quorum failure

mitigation_automation: circuit-break on write error; escalate to human ops for manual log recovery
reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-009 budgets 500ms p95 for panel-action writes. Immutable audit trail (REQ012) is non-negotiable; any lost action = non-defensible decision. Distributed log (Kafka 3-node quorum) requires replication ACK from 2-of-3 replicas. Low-latency LAN (<10ms) is assumed; WAN failover breaks budget. Multi-year cold retention supports incident investigation and regulatory audit (AAIB/Service Inquiry).
recommendation: Monitor Kafka broker health: in-sync-replicas count, replication lag, fsync latency. Alert if ISR < 2 (quorum at risk). Track panel action write frequency per panel; expect 30-50 actions/sec peak during voting. Alert if write error rate exceeds 0.01% (1 in 10,000 writes failing).

monitoring.alert_warning: Any classification boundary reject (evidence cannot be classified at target tier); audit escalation required
monitoring.alert_critical: Reject rate > 0.1% of access attempts; possible access-control bypass attempt or data corruption
monitoring.alert_incident: Successful cross-tier access (OFFICIAL user accessing SECRET data); security event

mitigation_automation: block access on any reject; escalate to CISO
reasoning: Classification boundary is a hard security control (REQ018). Every tier transition (OFFICIAL → OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE, etc.) must be audited. Rejects indicate either legitimate access denial (expected) or security anomaly. REQ018 absent in original requirements but critical for MOD-involved trials; monitor both success rate and rejection patterns. Classification-marked at highest tier (SECRET) per parent story DoD.
recommendation: Log all tier-transition attempts with success/fail status, user ID, evidence ID, source/target tier. Alert on reject rate spike. Generate daily classification boundary audit report: total transitions, reject counts, user patterns. Test monthly: attempt to access SECRET evidence from OFFICIAL-tier user (should reject instantly).

monitoring.alert_warning: Library revision lag > 7 days (clause update not deployed to CDN edge); regulator using stale guidance
monitoring.alert_critical: Library revision lag > 14 days; regulator risk of applying obsolete clause to decision
monitoring.alert_incident: Library versioning broken; revision ID mismatch between cache and source of truth

mitigation_automation: auto-invalidate CDN cache if source library version advances
reasoning: Parent story DoD requires clause-library versioning. Every decision record pins library revision ID at time of decision (audit trail). Drift monitor detects stale clauses in production (inspectors using outdated guidance). Quarterly library updates (3-4 major, 1-2 minor per quarter per Ada capacity model) → freshness check essential. Alert if library revision in production lags source by >7 days (allows for staged rollout, but flags slow propagation).
recommendation: Track library revision ID in code + evidence store. On library update, measure time to: (1) CDN edge cache, (2) all inspector client caches. Alert if any inspector sees >7-day-old revision. Generate weekly report: library version distribution across inspectors (ensure no old versions in active use).

monitoring.alert_warning: Pass rate declining (>10% relative drop week-over-week); possible regression in applicant evidence quality OR readiness-check validation too strict
monitoring.alert_critical: >50% of submissions fail readiness check on first attempt; validation rules broken OR applicants confused
monitoring.alert_incident: Pass/fail verdict unavailable (readiness engine crashes); submissions stuck in limbo

mitigation_automation: if pass rate drops >30% in 1 hour, escalate to CAA case officers for manual review of recent rejections
reasoning: Parent story metric: 'Full workbench open-and-review cycle shall not require more than two working days.' Readiness Check is gate; high failure rate means applicants re-do work (cycle time increases). KPI: track pass % over rolling 7-day window. Pass/fail verdict also logs blocking issues (via telemetry) to understand quality trends (e.g., 'ConOps missing in 40% of submissions' → training opportunity).
recommendation: Publish daily readiness report: pass/fail counts, median time to gate pass, top-10 blocking issues. Correlate with clause-library revisions (did readiness rule change break applicant expectations?). Quarterly retrospective: analyze submissions that initially failed but later passed (rework effort). Track CSAT: how many applicants report readiness feedback helpful?

monitoring.alert_warning: Regulator override rate on AI flags > 40% over last 10 cases; possible AI model drift or regulator threshold shift
monitoring.alert_critical: Override rate > 60% for single regulator; AI flags unreliable for this reviewer OR calibration training needed
monitoring.alert_incident: Cohen's kappa inter-rater reliability <0.7 on held-out calibration cases; panel decisions not sufficiently aligned (§23 obligation)

mitigation_automation: trigger mandatory calibration session if override rate >40% for individual regulator
reasoning: Workbench AI-flag narrative service (NFR-MON-WB-004) generates RED/AMBER/GREEN flags on evidence. Regulators may accept or override. High override rate signals: (1) model drift (evidence distribution changed), (2) regulator threshold shift (acceptance criteria evolved), or (3) model untrained on new case types. REQ009 ML Assurance Case + parent story DoD require calibration loop. Track per-regulator and per-case-type (BVLOS vs autonomy vs swarm, etc.).
recommendation: Log every flag + override decision. Quarterly calibration: present 10 held-out cases to all panelists independently, measure inter-rater agreement (Cohen's kappa). If kappa <0.7, conduct re-training workshop. Generate per-regulator override-rate dashboard: trend line, comparison to panel average, flags where this regulator uniquely differs from peers.

component: Workbench AI components drift (feature + prediction drift)

monitoring.alert_warning: Evidence input distribution shifted (evidence pack composition changing); model operates outside training ODD
monitoring.alert_critical: AI prediction distribution shifted >20% (e.g., flag rate was 30%, now 50%); model retraining required
monitoring.alert_incident: Model confidence dropped <60% median; predictions unreliable

mitigation_automation: hold inference results pending review if confidence <60% on any evidence chunk
reasoning: Parent story DoD: 'Workbench AI components drift monitor.' Coverage matrix engine (NFR-MON-WB-003) and narrative service (NFR-MON-WB-004) are ML-based. Monitor: (1) input distribution (evidence content), (2) model outputs (flag distribution), (3) confidence scores. Drift signals retrain requirement. GCAP/Tempest trials may introduce new evidence types (advanced autonomy scenarios) → model must adapt.
recommendation: Collect feature statistics per case: document types (PDF vs. video), evidence length distribution, clause topic distribution. Monthly: compare to training-set baseline. Alert if Kolmogorov-Smirnov test p-value < 0.05 (distribution shift detected). Track model confidence percentiles (p50, p90, p99). If p50 confidence trends down >10% month-over-month, trigger retraining evaluation.

component: DAA encounter rate and resolution latency

monitoring.alert_warning: Encounter resolution latency > 4.5s (90% of 5s budget for cooperative encounters); close to safety margin
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 encounter latency > 5s for cooperative OR > 27s for non-cooperative (DO-365 compliance risk)
monitoring.alert_incident: Encounter detection failure (sensor fusion miss); swarm did not respond to nearby aircraft

mitigation_automation: escalate to trial safety officer if encounter latency >3s (early warning)
reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-012 budgets 5s p95 for cooperative DAA encounter resolution, 30s for non-cooperative. Safety-critical: collision avoidance. APM instruments ground-control state machine (sensor fusion latency, consensus coordination, command generation). Per-event logging captures every encounter for post-trial forensic analysis. Retains for 7 years (regulatory investigation support).
recommendation: Log all DAA events: detected aircraft type (cooperative vs non-cooperative), bearing, range, altitude, resolution maneuver, latency. Track encounter rate per trial (1-2/hour typical per Ada capacity). Alert if any encounter takes >6s to resolve (>20% over budget margin). Monthly: analyze encounter resolution patterns; identify any trends in latency.

component: C2 link SNR, jitter, packet loss

monitoring.alert_warning: Packet loss rate > 2% over 1-minute window (elevated but recoverable); C2 robustness tested
monitoring.alert_critical: Packet loss > 5% sustained for 10 seconds OR SNR < 3 dB; trial safety margin eroded
monitoring.alert_incident: C2 link loss detected; autonomous survival mode triggered

mitigation_automation: activate C2 link redundancy (satellite backup) if loss rate >5% for >5 sec
reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-013 budgets 1000ms p95 C2 round-trip. Packet loss and jitter directly degrade latency. Monitor RF link quality (SNR via SDR telemetry, jitter via timestamp analysis). LTE/5G networks are inherently variable; alert thresholds reflect acceptable operational envelope per REQ006. Link loss triggers autonomous survival (geofence hold, RTL).
recommendation: Log SNR, RSSI, packet loss rate every 100ms during trial. Plot C2 link quality timeline per trial (identify geographic dead zones, time-of-day patterns). Alert if SNR drops <0 dB (link marginal). Correlate packet loss with encounter events (busy periods may see higher loss due to bandwidth contention). Post-trial: generate C2 link quality report for trial debrief.

monitoring.alert_warning: Any aircraft distance-to-geofence-edge < 100m; approaching boundary (late-stage warning before breach)
monitoring.alert_critical: Distance-to-edge < 50m for any aircraft in swarm; geofence containment margin very tight
monitoring.alert_incident: Geofence breach detected (GPS position outside authorized flight envelope); automatic containment triggered

mitigation_automation: automatic containment maneuver if breach occurs (mechanical failsafe per REQ005)
reasoning: REQ005 'Automatic Containment' requires geofence monitoring. Distance-to-edge distribution reveals: (1) swarm flying too close to boundary (operational risk), (2) GPS noise (multipath errors), (3) dynamic geofence shrinkage (MOD range safety requirement). Monitor per-vehicle and swarm-average distance. Alert on breach (emergency condition requiring incident response).
recommendation: Log GPS position + calculated distance-to-edge every 1 second. Generate per-trial geofence histogram: % time at <50m, <100m, <500m from boundary. Alert if any vehicle distance < 0 (impossible, signals GPS/geofence mismatch). Post-trial: overlay flight path on authorized geofence map; identify any close-calls or boundary-hugging behavior (training feedback for pilot).

monitoring.alert_warning: Consensus cycle duration trending >150ms (90% of 200ms budget); coordination protocol slowing
monitoring.alert_critical: Consensus cycle > 200ms OR any aircraft consensus failure (node isolation); swarm decision-making stalled
monitoring.alert_incident: Consensus quorum lost (<50% of swarm synchronized); formation breaks or returns to manual C2

mitigation_automation: revert to manual C2 control if consensus quorum lost >10 seconds
reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-014 budgets 200ms p95 for swarm consensus. Inter-vehicle coordination via gossip/broadcast; consensus time increases with swarm size (O(n²) pairwise comparisons). Monitor: (1) cycle duration per decision heartbeat, (2) agreement rate (% of vehicles reached consensus), (3) decision latency (time from sensor input to coordinated action). Consensus failure = swarm splits into independent actors (loss of coordinated autonomy).
recommendation: Log consensus start/end timestamp, participating aircraft count, decision output (e.g., 'all maintain formation' vs 'Aircraft-2 break right'). Alert if cycle duration exceeds 250ms (timeout margin). Monthly: analyze consensus performance across different swarm sizes (5 vs 10 vehicles); verify linear-or-better scaling. Test consensus resilience: simulate single aircraft communication loss, measure recovery time.

component: Battery state of charge and predicted time-to-contingency

monitoring.alert_warning: Predicted time-to-RTL < 10 minutes for any aircraft (margin tightening); crew plan recovery soon
monitoring.alert_critical: Predicted time-to-RTL < 5 minutes OR battery < 20% SOC; imminent forced landing risk
monitoring.alert_incident: Battery completely depleted mid-flight; aircraft unable to execute RTL (emergency recovery)

mitigation_automation: auto-trigger RTL if time-to-contingency < 3 minutes
reasoning: Safety-critical: battery depletion ends flight abruptly. Model discharge rate in real-time accounting for compute load (autonomy + DAA inference), wind conditions, vehicle weight. Predict time-to-contingency: time remaining to reach safe landing zone from current position. Alert if crew cannot execute planned recovery (forces early RTL). Per-vehicle monitoring; also swarm-level alert if any vehicle is contingency-limited (bottleneck for swarm).
recommendation: Log battery SOC, voltage, current draw every 1 second per aircraft. Calculate discharge rate (amps per hour); compare to pre-flight plan. Alert if actual discharge >10% above predicted (thermal throttling, headwind, or load increase). Generate per-trial battery report: actual vs predicted discharge, time-to-RTL history, any near-contingency events. Post-flight: debrief on power management (did crew manage intelligently, or was power budget misestimated?).

component: Onboard ML inference latency and confidence distribution

monitoring.alert_warning: Onboard ML p95 latency > 180ms (90% of 200ms budget); approaching autonomy-decision latency budget
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 ML latency > 200ms OR inference error rate > 2%; autonomy decision timing at risk
monitoring.alert_incident: Model inference crash; autonomy fallback activated (manual C2 or pre-programmed behavior)

mitigation_automation: revert to fallback behavior if inference latency >250ms or confidence <50%
reasoning: Noyce NFR-LAT-015 budgets 200ms p95 for onboard ML inference (e.g., next-waypoint selection, DAA evasion). Jetson Orin or similar provides GPU inference; onboard compute is TDP-constrained. Monitor: (1) latency per inference call, (2) model confidence (%), (3) inference error rate (exceptions/NaN outputs). Confidence <60% signals ODD boundary: model operating on unseen inputs.
recommendation: Log every ML inference: input features, latency, output, confidence score. Alert if latency >250ms (hard deadline for control loop). Track confidence distribution per flight (histogram). Alert if median confidence trends down (model degradation). Post-trial: cluster inference examples by confidence to identify which scenarios model is uncertain about (training data needs).

monitoring.alert_warning: State machine p95 latency > 90ms (90% of 100ms budget); swarm decision validation slowing
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 100ms OR state-machine error rate > 0.5%; autonomy compliance validation broken
monitoring.alert_incident: State machine crash or stall; ground control loses real-time autonomy visibility

mitigation_automation: escalate to human operator (trial abort) if state machine unresponsive >5 seconds
reasoning: REQ008 Swarm Decision-Making requires real-time ground validation: did swarm comply with authorized flight plan? Ground-control state machine processes telemetry from swarm (25-200 msg/sec), validates each message against authorized plan, logs state transitions. Latency budget: <100ms (must complete validation before next message arrives). Critical for regulatory oversight (CAA/MOD must prove swarm did not deviate).
recommendation: Log state machine latency per telemetry message (histogram). Track state-machine CPU utilization; alert if >80% (approaching capacity). Alert if validation error rate exceeds 1 per 10,000 messages (1-in-10k validations fail). Monthly: analyze state-machine logs for compliance violations (instances where swarm deviated from authorized plan; require human investigation).

monitoring.alert_warning: Evidence store GetObject latency > 90ms (trending toward 100ms SLA); S3 performance degrading
monitoring.alert_critical: GetObject latency > 100ms p95; metadata retrieval for case assembly may miss SLA
monitoring.alert_incident: Evidence store unavailable (bucket access denied or S3 outage); cases cannot load

mitigation_automation: activate CloudFront cache for frequently accessed evidence; pre-warm before panel sessions
reasoning: NFR-LAT-006 case-open budgets 1500ms for evidence-metadata-load. S3 GetObject latency is part of that. Monitor object-storage read performance per tier (OFFICIAL faster, SECRET/STRAP may be slower due to encryption/compliance). Track storage growth (34-month projection: 140 GB per Ada capacity). Availability is high; focus is on latency consistency.
recommendation: Monitor S3 bucket metrics: GetObject latency p50/p95/p99, PutObject latency, request counts. Alert if GetObject p95 > 100ms sustained. Track storage utilization growth; ensure growth projections remain on track (may reveal unexpected case volume). Test quarterly: simulate S3 partition (brief degradation); verify graceful fallback and recovery.

component: Primary database (write throughput and read latency)

monitoring.alert_warning: Write latency p95 > 400ms (trending toward 500ms panel-action-write budget); master becoming bottleneck
monitoring.alert_critical: Write latency p95 > 500ms sustained OR write error rate > 0.1%; SLA breach for audit trail
monitoring.alert_incident: Database unavailable; cases cannot be loaded or decisions recorded

mitigation_automation: add DB read replica if read latency >100ms; scale master vertically if write latency >400ms
reasoning: NFR-LAT-009 panel-action log write budgets 500ms p95. Database master handles all writes (audit trail, case state, panel decisions). Monitor: (1) write TPS (transactions/second), (2) write latency percentiles, (3) replication lag to read replicas, (4) lock-contention events. At 100 concurrent users, expect 80 write TPS + 400 read TPS.
recommendation: Enable CloudWatch/DataDog monitoring: WriteThroughput, ReadThroughput, WriteLatency, ReadLatency, DatabaseConnections. Alert if WriteLatency p95 > 400ms (warning) or >500ms (critical). Track replication lag; alert if lag >1s (read replicas becoming stale). Monthly: analyze slow-query logs; identify any N+1 query patterns or missing indexes.

component: Web front-end and BFF (request latency and error rate)

monitoring.alert_warning: Frontend p95 latency > 1800ms (trending toward 2s applicant usability threshold); DOM rendering or JS execution slowing
monitoring.alert_critical: p95 > 2000ms sustained OR error rate > 1%; user-facing quality degradation
monitoring.alert_incident: Frontend service unavailable (CDN or origin down); applicants cannot access portal

mitigation_automation: auto-scale web tier if CPU >70% OR request rate >500 req/s per instance
reasoning: RUM monitors real user experience: download, parsing, rendering, JavaScript execution. Noyce NFR-LAT-001 budgets 3s p95 end-to-end for applicant workspace creation; frontend is part of that. Monitor per-page and per-feature: upload page, dashboard, evidence nav, etc. Applicant portal is high-attack-surface (public-facing); error rate may indicate WAF blocking or DDoS.
recommendation: Inject RUM beacon on page load complete. Segment by device type (mobile vs desktop), network speed (broadband vs 4G). Track Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS). Alert if error rate > 1% (HTTP 4xx/5xx from origin). Monitor CDN cache hit rate; alert if <90% (cache churn or expired TTL).
